steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "Relatively few of these particles are likely to reach Earth's ______ since the meteoroids' compression of the air surrounding them causes the vast majority to burn up in the mesosphere." The word 'however' must be inserted at the blank as a transitional parenthetical within this sentence — not between two independent clauses.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — surface, however;: Places a semicolon after 'however' but only a comma before it. A semicolon here implies what follows is an independent clause, but 'since' introduces a dependent clause. Asymmetric punctuation around the parenthetical. ✗
  • Choice B — surface, however,: Commas on both sides of 'however' correctly set it off as a parenthetical adverb within the sentence. The 'since' clause that follows is properly treated as dependent. ✓
  • Choice C — surface; however,: The semicolon before 'however' signals two independent clauses. But the clause after the blank begins with 'since,' making it dependent — a semicolon cannot precede a dependent clause. ✗
  • Choice D — surface. However,: A period ends the sentence, leaving 'since the meteoroids' compression...' as a fragment on its own. ✗
B is correct.
